Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing

Items.Key: off-by-one error in safe_string_copy|off-by-one error|cwe-193|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|202|205|string_utils::safe_string_copy|size_t src_len = strlen(src); if (src_len > dest_size) src_len = dest_size; memcpy(dest, src, src_len); dest[src_len] = '\0';|if `src_len` is set equal to `dest_size`, `memcpy` fills `dest` perfectly, but `dest[src_len] = '\0'` writes one byte outside the intended buffer boundary.|providing a source string whose length is greater than or equal to `dest_size` and calling the function.|decrease the limit: `if (src_len > dest_size - 1) src_len = dest_size - 1;` to reserve space for the null terminator.->off-by-one error in safe_string_copy|off-by-one error|cwe-193|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|202|205|string_utils::safe_string_copy|size_t src_len = strlen(src); if (src_len > dest_size) src_len = dest_size; memcpy(dest, src, src_len); dest[src_len] = '\0';|if `src_len` is set equal to `dest_size`, `memcpy` fills `dest` perfectly, but `dest[src_len] = '\0'` writes one byte outside the intended buffer boundary.|providing a source string whose length is greater than or equal to `dest_size` and calling the function.|decrease the limit: `if (src_len > dest_size - 1) src_len = dest_size - 1;` to reserve space for the null terminator.

truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 reveals successful detection of four vulnerability categories (Command Injection, Format String, Heap Overflow, and Division by Zero) and two components of the stack buffer overflow sequence, with notable omissions of race conditions and memory errors.

Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the call to printf utilizing active_format, including the logic for setting the format from log_format.
Run 2 failed to identify the integer overflow in the factorial computation (result *= i).
Run 2 missed the use-after-free in the memory-pool cleanup involving deferred history flushing.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file;
int result = system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Run 2 accurately audited the config validation logic that builds a command from attacker-influenced configuration values.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: the code executes a raw strcpy (incorrectly prioritized) instead of a bound-checked copy, leading to a heap/stack overflow.
Items.Notes: This covers the input processing buffer overflow (line 831). Finding #5 in Run 2 captures the secondary boundary checks mentioned in the SC-V3-005 anchors.
Run 2 missed the race condition in the Thread-Pool Counter (ThreadSafeCounter increment logic).
Run 2 did not identify the logic bomb/emergency override in the auth logic.
Run 2 failed to see the path traversal in config folder concatenation.
Run 2 identified the incorrect strcpy on the parser's 256-byte expression buffer heap allocation.
Run 2 missed the hardcoded admin secret.
Run 2 did not audit the weak PRNG (mt19937 over 1000-9999) for session tokens.
Run 2 missed the thread-unsafe use of localtime in the timestamp cache.
Run 2 missed the execution of scripts on paths constructed from potentially unsafe logic involving the log file.
Run 2 missed findings related to persistent session state logging/state.
Run 2 missed handles for potential exceptions in the variable set command context.
Run 2 did not identify the integer underflow in the memory pool block split logic.
Run 2 failed to identify unbounded recursion in recursive pars.
Run 2 did not identify the memory data race in the result outcomecache.
Run 2 missed the race condition (TOCTOU) in the filesystem existence check logic.
Run 2 successfully identified the specific integer division by zero in the route score calculation.
